description: Same school play from newsletter mention vs direct invite - harder to match

content_parts.text:
You are comparing calendar events to detect duplicates.

**New Event:**
- Title: Spring Musical: The Wizard of Oz
- Start: 2026-04-25T19:00:00
- End: 2026-04-25T21:00:00
- Location: Lincoln Elementary Auditorium
- Description: Your child's class is performing! Tickets available at the door, $5 per person.

**Existing Events (same date):**

1. Event ID: event-001
   - Title: School Play - Wizard of Oz
   - Start: 2026-04-25T19:00:00
   - End: 2026-04-25T21:00:00
   - Location: Lincoln Elementary
   - Description: Annual spring performance by 3rd and 4th graders

2. Event ID: event-002
   - Title: PTA Meeting
   - Start: 2026-04-24T18:30:00
   - End: 2026-04-24T20:00:00
   - Location: Lincoln Elementary Library
   - Description: Monthly PTA meeting - all parents welcome

3. Event ID: event-003
   - Title: Soccer Practice
   - Start: 2026-04-25T16:00:00
   - End: 2026-04-25T17:30:00
   - Location: Community Field
   - Description: Regular Friday practice

**Question:** Is the new event the same as any of the existing events?

Consider events the same if they refer to the same real-world event, even if:
- Wording is slightly different
- One has more details than the other
- Time changed slightly (updates)

Return the matching Event ID (or null if no match) and brief reasoning.

trace.raw_response_text: {"matched_event_id": "event-001", "reasoning": "The new event has the same start and end time (19:00-21:00) on 2026-04-25 as event-001. The location is effectively the same (Lincoln Elementary Auditorium is part of Lincoln Elementary). The titles both refer to a Wizard of Oz performance, and the descriptions describe the same school event (with the new event providing more specific details like tickets). The event times and core content match, so it is considered the same event."}
